repo.description: Vim-fork focused on extensibility and usability
repo.name: neovim

body:
Moves `stdpath("log")` to `stdpath("state")/logs`, instead of being equivalent to `stdpath("state")`.

Implements `:log`, a command for easily opening log files in `stdpath("log")`:
- Provides a convenient way to view logs (short and cross-platform command, automatic `G`, tab completion).
- [Missing `:LspLog`](https://github.com/neovim/nvim-lspconfig/pull/4234#issuecomment-3661989287) command from nvim-lspconfig becomes `:log lsp`.
- Standardized method for plugins to provide logs.

Open questions during review:
- Should this command be named `:log` or something else?
- Should `:log` by itself open the `stdpath("log")` directory, or should it be an alias for `:log nvim`?

Closes #35082

comment_data.body: I think iter is not available in core: https://github.com/neovim/neovim/blob/ae2829ee35c1fb8649f9d4046c5af48987193d03/src/nvim/CMakeLists.txt#L642-L652
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -186,4 +187,38 @@ function M.lsp_complete(line)
   end
 end
 
+--- @type string
+--- @diagnostic disable-next-line: assign-type-mismatch
+local log_dir = vim.fn.stdpath('log')
+
+--- Implements command: `:editlog {file}`.
+--- @param filename string
+M.ex_editlog = function(filename)
+  if filename == '' then
+    vim.cmd.edit { log_dir }
+  else
+    local path = fs.joinpath(log_dir, filename .. '.log')
+    if not vim.uv.fs_stat(path) then
+      echo_err(("No such log file: '%s'"):format(path))
+      return
+    end
+    vim.cmd.edit { path }
+    vim.cmd.normal { 'G', bang = true }
+  end
+end
+
+--- Completion logic for `:editlog` command
+--- @return string[] list of completions
+function M.editlog_complete()
+  return vim
+    .iter(fs.dir(log_dir))

comment_data.body:
Understood. I'll write it out manually then. Thanks.

I'll make sure to include a lack of VIMRUNTIME in the tests I write.

comment_data.body: followup/non-blocker: maybe also accept fullpath and literal filename. If for whatever reason someone pastes in the filename or tries `:log $NVIM_LOG_FILE` ...
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -1733,4 +1733,27 @@ mark a file as trusted or untrusted using the |:trust| command or the
 			or |vim.secure.read()|, the user will be prompted to
 			trust or deny it.
 
+==============================================================================
+13. Log Files							*log-files*
+
+Nvim keeps log files in `stdpath("log")`. See |standard-path| for where that
+location is on your system.
+
+							*:log*
+:log [logname]
+			Open a log file that exists in `stdpath("log")`.
+			`[logname]` is the name of the file with the extension
+			".log" removed.

comment_data.body: this and `wrapped_edit` should probably be in `_core/util.lua` so we can start thinking about more places where they might be wanted, and maybe eventually lifting into stdlib.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -186,4 +186,71 @@ function M.lsp_complete(line)
   end
 end
 
+--- @type string
+--- @diagnostic disable-next-line: assign-type-mismatch
+local log_dir = vim.fn.stdpath('log')
+
+--- Gets a buffer by name, so it can be reused if one with the same name exists
+--- @param name string
+--- @return integer?
+local function get_buf_by_name(name)

comment_data.body: should prefer `error()` unless there is an associated `EXX` error code.
comment_data.diff_hunk:
@@ -186,4 +186,71 @@ function M.lsp_complete(line)
   end
 end
 
+--- @type string
+--- @diagnostic disable-next-line: assign-type-mismatch
+local log_dir = vim.fn.stdpath('log')
+
+--- Gets a buffer by name, so it can be reused if one with the same name exists
+--- @param name string
+--- @return integer?
+local function get_buf_by_name(name)
+  for _, buf in ipairs(api.nvim_list_bufs()) do
+    if api.nvim_buf_get_name(buf) == name then
+      return buf
+    end
+  end
+end
+
+--- :edit, but it respects commands like :hor, :vert, :tab, etc.
+local function wrapped_edit(file, mods_str)
+  local cmdline = table.concat({ mods_str, 'log' }, ' ')
+  local mods = api.nvim_parse_cmd(cmdline, {}).mods
+  --- @diagnostic disable-next-line: need-check-nil
+  if mods.tab > 0 or mods.split ~= '' or mods.horizontal or mods.vertical then
+    local buf = get_buf_by_name(file)
+    if buf == nil then
+      buf = api.nvim_create_buf(true, false)
+    end
+    vim.cmd.sbuffer { buf, mods = mods }
+  end
+  vim.cmd.edit { file }
+end
+
+--- Implements command: `:log {file}`.
+--- @param filename string
+--- @param mods string
+M.ex_log = function(filename, mods)
+  if filename == '' then
+    wrapped_edit(log_dir, mods)
+  else
+    local path --- @type string
+    -- Special case for NVIM_LOG_FILE
+    local nvim_log_file = vim.env.NVIM_LOG_FILE --- @type string
+    if filename == 'nvim' and nvim_log_file and nvim_log_file ~= '' then
+      path = nvim_log_file
+    else
+      path = fs.joinpath(log_dir, filename .. '.log')
+    end
+    if not vim.uv.fs_stat(path) then
+      echo_err(("No such log file: '%s'"):format(path))

comment_data.body: off topic: we should find a way to further abstract Lua-implemented Ex commands so that this boilerplate isn't needed...
I wanted to give it a EXX code but I didn't know how to create a localized string in lua. Is there a way to do this?

comment_regular_data.body: Renaming `:editlog` to `:log` due to the reasoning [here](https://github.com/neovim/neovim/issues/35082#issuecomment-4145852724).

This PR is now ready for review!

In addition to the typical level of review, I recommend paying extra attention to my C code, as I do not know C yet and just made my best guesses. @zeertzjq especially you may want to take a look to make sure I haven't done anything unsafe.

It would also be great if one of the reviewers gave this PR the proper labels. Thanks!

>  Should `:log` by itself open the `stdpath("log")` directory,

I like that idea. Or maybe `:log .` ? 😅
After the `_core/util.lua` change we can merge this, thanks for driving it @superatomic !

> If we go with `:log` for opening a log file, what's the plan for changing log levels?

I really think we should repurpose 'verbose' for that. Will require adjusting the current definitions of (the first ~5) 'verbose' levels, but that's fine imo.

title: feat(logs,ex)!: move logs to `stdpath("state")/logs` and add `:log` command
